text: 35. A Book Fair You Visited / Visiting A Book Fair Every year in February, I visit the Ekushe Book Fair, which is officially known as Amar Ekushey Book Fair. Like the previous years, I have visited the Ekushey Boi Mela. The fair is organized by the Bangla Academy to commemorate the historic Language Movement. The fair is held in the premises of Bangla Academy in Dhaka and attracts thousands of book lovers from all over the country. When I entered the fair, I saw beautifully decorated stalls filled with new books of different genres such as novels, poems, essays, research works, and children’s literature. Famous writers come to the fair to meet readers and sign autographs, which creates great excitement among visitors. The environment of the fair is very lively and inspiring. People of all ages, especially students and young readers, gather there with great enthusiasm. I bought several books written by my favourite authors and also enjoyed cultural programs arranged in the evening. The fair reminds us of the sacrifices made by the martyrs of the Language Movement in 1952. It not only promotes our literature and culture but also encourages people to develop the habit of reading books. Visiting the Ekushe Book Fair was a memorable and enriching experience for me.
searchable: paragraph CQ সাধারণ 35. A Book Fair You Visited / Visiting A Book Fair Every year in February, I visit the Ekushe Book Fair, which is officially known as Amar Ekushey Book Fair. Like the previous years, I have visited the Ekushey Boi Mela. The fair is organized by the Bangla Academy to commemorate the historic Language Movement. The fair is held in the premises of Bangla Academy in Dhaka and attracts thousands of book lovers from all over the country. When I entered the fair, I saw beautifully decorated stalls filled with new books of different genres such as novels, poems, essays, research works, and children’s literature. Famous writers come to the fair to meet readers and sign autographs, which creates great excitement among visitors. The environment of the fair is very lively and inspiring. People of all ages, especially students and young readers, gather there with great enthusiasm. I bought several books written by my favourite authors and also enjoyed cultural programs arranged in the evening. The fair reminds us of the sacrifices made by the martyrs of the Language Movement in 1952. It not only promotes our literature and culture but also encourages people to develop the habit of reading books. Visiting the Ekushe Book Fair was a memorable and enriching experience for me.

text: 11. Science in everyday life A journey you  have enjoyed / A memorable event. A railway journey is always an interesting and pleasant experience in life. I have travelled by train many times on different occasions. But some journeys remain vivid in life and cannot be vanished from memory. The journey that I made during the last summer vacation was one of the memorable events in my life. I feel excited to describe it. The news while visiting my uncle's house in Chittagong pleased me heavily. My uncle is a government employee who has been living there since last five years. My parents decided to visit the place by train and it filled my heart with joy. The date was fixed on the day of first holiday. My father bought five tickets of the Intercity Express. Our Journey started from the kamlapur Rail station. The station was very nice. There was great noise all around. We reached the station just before the train left. As the train arrived, the passengers were rushing towards the compartments. I observed some passengers were sitting on the roof. They all were in a cheerful mind. We all were ready. When first bell rang before the starting of the train all the passengers seemed to remain alert so that nothing was left behind. Finally, the train left the Kamalapur station. We heaved a sigh of relief after starting the train. The train halted at every station. It ran between cities, bazars, green fields, valleys hills and forests. The train was always in high speed. The distant trees seemed to move in the opposite direction. But the sights that attracted me most on the way were that of the green fields, valleys, hills and the even green forest of Bangladesh. Besides the red glow of the sun and the flying birds all around provided the charming scene to me. I could have a good look outside. I enjoyed myself with scene the farmers  were working in the field. The green fields and the yellow-colored paddy fields, the valley of the hills and the trees,and  the forests fascinated me profusely. I felt thrilled every moment. There were friendly passengers all around and I talked with some of them. Luckily, I got a good seat near window. One special thing impressed me very much was that the people in the train waved their hands to say good bye to their relatives and friends on the platform at every station that came to see them off. As I was on the side of a window, I could enjoy a cool breeze and see the natural scenery outside. But the people inside the train were busy reading news papers and magazines or talking with others on the current political and social events of the country. After about ten hours of Journey, the train came to a halt at Chittagong station. We had at last reached our destination. We got down and walked a short distance and reached my uncle's house. In conclusion, I may say that the eventful journey was a very pleasant one. I can never forget this journey. The reminiscences of this journey often flicker in my mind every now and then. It was, by far, the most unforgettable journey of my life that has left its deep impression on the canvas of my life and this is the most  memorable event in my life. Duties of a student A student is a person who devotes himself to the pursuit of knowledge and learning. Today's Students are the future citizen of the country. They can only take the challenge of the coming days, if the students are sincere and they acquire the education in real sense. It is therefore, the first and foremost duty of a student is to seek knowledge and prepare himself with good qualities that a student should acquire. The students constitute a great potential force capable of leading a nation to the greatest heights of progress. So, the future of every nation depends much on their students. The students should consider this fact with sincere and they should improve their intellect and widen their mental capabilities through acquiring knowledge. A student should be punctual in attending classes, where he learns the good traits of life such as, discipline, obedience, dutifulness, diligences, respect to elders, honesty, patriotism and other qualities to lead the nation towards development, peace and prosperity. It is the duty of every student to acquire these fine qualities of life which will enable him/her to stand in good stead in the future. Another important duty of a student is to prepare himself for the career he wants to pursue. It is in school life that the future career takes roots. During school life, one becomes aware of one's abilities, limitations and different professions available. Therefore, during this stage he should make up his mind as to what he should become in future. A student has a duty to fight against all evils existing in the society. He has a tremendous responsibility to stand against injustice, inequality, oppression, exploitation, corruption, criminalization of politics, communalism, casteism, misappropriation of public money etc. The students also have a great responsibilities to help the less fortunate members of the society by way of social work. They can offer great services by teaching illiterate children of the society. The students should devote themselves in creating public awareness on various social and political issues. A student can also render valuable services to the nation at the time of calamities such as, floods, earthquakes, severe drought and accidents. The students should be ready to perform valuable services to the cause of the nation. They should stand by the country at the time of national emergencies. A good student should take part in games and sports and other extra-curricular activities to make himself fully prepared to face the challenges in the years to come. In this regard, a student should be punctual, hard working and dutiful and a man with moral character and high vision. In conclusion, we should focus on the fact that the prime and foremost duty of a student is to seek knowledge and wisdom and he must gladly submit to the rules of discipline to be a good citizen of the country. He should be symbol of love and a real patriot. If the students are not aware of the essential qualities that a student should acquire at the right time, the country cannot expect much  from a student. The students should remember it and perform their duties accordingly. Discipline Discipline is a method of training of one's mind or body or of controlling one's behavior. It also refers to order or code of conduct. It is a systematic method of obtaining obedience. It is absolutely necessary for peace, progress, prosperity of an individual as well as the society. It is a process and controlled behavior that strengthens law and order in the society. Discipline has its own value and influence of all spheres of life. Life without self discipline is no life. As it is a set/rules, we need to be guided by rules and regulations. Life without discipline is bound to go astray and is totally meaningless. With proper discipline, an individual or a country can be led in a right track. Where there is no discipline, there is no progress and no success. Discipline brings peace, progress and tranquility in our life and country. If we want to keep our country free from political turmoil and social unrest, every citizens must obey the rules and laws of the country. It must be observed and maintained in the political offairs and activities. Otherwise, political conflict and the conflict of interest may exist in the political process that may lead the whole nation in an untoward situation. There is value of discipline in all walks of life. Whether at school or at home, in the office or in the factory, in play ground or in the battlefield discipline is a must. Without discipline, there must have complete chaos, unrest and disorder all the while. There will be no peace and stability if discipline is not maintained. For example, in school if the boys are not disciplined, the boys themselves will learn nothing and the very purpose of education will be incompleted. Similarly, if the subordinates do not obey their senior officers in offices and factories, it will become difficult to run the administrative activities and there will be no production there. Discipline in the army and the law and order agencies i.. very remarkable. If the soldiers don't carry out the orders of their commanders and generals, the whole system within its structure will be collapsed and they will not be established as an effective force. The police is the members of law and order agencies. If the police refuses to carry out their duties and responsibilities, and if they are not led by their organizational rules and regulations, the law and order situation of the country will be disrupted and public life will be abnormal. Nature shows a shining example of discipline. The sun, the moon and all the planets of the universe follow discipline strictly. We also see the living example a of discipline in ants, bees and other living being of our environment. In conclusion, we may say that discipline is the secret of success. Life and society without discipline are absolutely meaningless. Without discipline, life will be conflicting, hazardous and ineffective. So discipline must be maintained everywhere. The higher is the sense of disciple, the better it is the people and the country. Tree Plantation Tree plantation is a process of planting trees-seedling generally for conservation of forest and land scalping   purposes. It is a key issue at this moment all over the world. Trees are one of the main elements working to save our environment. They are closely related to our life as well as the other animals. Plantation of trees is thus a crying need of the time. Trees are the best gift of nature. Nature decorates itself with trees showing eye-catering beauty every where in our planet. Trios expose not only beauty but also put immense benefit for human being and other living being in the world. So planting of trees has been given priority to increase the beauty of nature and put the environment in a better position.. Since the dawn of civilization, there were innumerable trees all over the world. But with the passage of time, men started cutting of trees indiscriminately exposing a threat to our environment. Now it is a global concern. People all over world are eager to plant more trees and like go with the campaign. "Grow more trees and save the environment". With the absence of required and sufficient trees, the world's temperature is getting warmer gradually., This situation has changed our climate and eco-system tremendously and caused green house effect. Only plantation of more trees can prevent the environment from pollution. Without oxygen we cannot live for a moment that we get from the tree. Trees take in carbon dioxide and produce oxygen. Carbon dioxide makes our atmosphere polluted. Trees consume this cardon dioxide and thereby makes our environment safe for us. Other than the aforesaid issues, trees came to our life in many ways. We get fruits, flowers, fire woods and timbers. Fruit bearing trees provide us with many types of fruits for our good health. They give us shade and reduce our tiredness. They are used as timber for making ey-catching furniture. Tree plantation draws attention of the people to the fact of environmental degradation all over the world. Due to lack of required forest land, the world experiences less rainfall, draught resulting desertification. The low-lying countries of the world like Bangladesh, Maldives, Sreelanka, have to fight against natural disasters like sidre, Ila, Nargis and other cyclones and floods. Thanks to atmospheric pollution, we should plant trees near our houses, villages and in the forest land for forestation with a view to creating new forest. We should also plant trees on the both sides of the roads. We should advise all to plant two trees, if they cut a sigle one. Generally, rainy season is the best time for tree plantation. Most of the tree plants thrive well in rainy season. But only planting the tree is not enough. Because, planted trees may be destroyed by animals or otherwise. So we should be very careful about the newly planted trees. In conclusion, We may say that the government and the private organizations. Should raise awareness of the people for tree plantation to address all the issues related to our existence. Besides, attempts should be made and environmental laws should be implemented and new law should be enacted to stop cutting trees so that we may get a polluted free environment and live in a better world. My Hobby/ Favourite Hobby/Traveling as a Hobby. Hobby involves activities that we do for pleasure during our free time. It brings joy happiness, and refreshes our mind and soul together. It is the interest of a person in anything other than his main career or pre -occupation. As a Student, my main duty is to acquire knowledge and wisdom. But I believe only educational institution cannot satisfy my thirst for knowledge and cannot give me much pleasure. So I like to do something extra. I have keen interest in traveling when I am out of academic activities. Hobby differs from man to man according to his sweet choice. There are many kinds of hobby such as gardening, stamp collecting, hiking, coin-collecting, photography, listening to music, writing poems and novels, drawing picture, traveling, reading and so forth. Among the aforesaid hobbies, I like traveling as it gives me much pleasure. It is my ultimate choice as a hobby. Other people think that traveling is an expensive hobby and time consuming one. But I am of the view regarding choice, though it costs much money, its intrinsic value compensates the financial loss. So I think I have the right choice. As I have no other choice but traveling as a hobby, I make a pleasant tour in and outside my country. When I get-time during dimmer vacation, winter vacation or after the final examination is over, I make long or short trip to home and abroad. The world is so big and there are so many places to visit. Each has a different beauty, various summer, culture, language, history and customs. The hobby of travelling is a hobby of enduring the beauty of Allah's creativity and skillfulness. I like to get the opportunity given by Allah by travelling everywhere whenever I can. Regarding travelling place, my first choice is to visit my country's most popular and historical places. Cox's Bazar, Kuakata, Rangamati, Paherpur, Maynamati etc are of my first priority. But when I get long vacation, I make special tour to foreign country's noted tourist spots with my family members. There is no doubt that a person gets more life experience by walking a mile than reading an entire book. By traveling, we can make new friends, get rid of day to day frustration, and get something new. That is to sayit gives me a great break from my routine and an opportunity to learn a new culture, old history and make life long friends. It opens up a new avenue to take pleasure in learning the other cultures and languages. This type of traveling opens up my mind from a particular bend of thinking. In conclusion, I may say that traveling, my favourite hobby is an excellent way to see new places and to get new knowledge, experiences about new culture. I am of the view that it is a perfect educational hobby as it helps me learn a lot of new things in life. It is both educative and entertaining. So I think I have the right choice in selecting favourite hobby and it is definitely traveling. Your Aim in Life, or, Your future Plan Aim in life is a series of events and feelings that happen in our mind when we grow up. It may come to us in any stage of our life. Future plan is that way which enables us to work in a planned way to reach the goal. Different people have different aims and plans. So I have a high ambition in life. The aim which I cherish in my mind is to serve the country and this my ultimate aim in life. To materialize the aim, I have decided to become a politician and social worker. Thanks to my great desire to serve the people, I need to educate myself for well preparation to make the plan a success. That is way I want to get the higher education from a famous educational institution in my country. I am fond of reading and writing. I will work hard to achieve knowledge and wisdom that I will apply in my practical life. I will devote myself to bring good result in my educational life. For this purpose, I do study regularly and participate in extra-curricular activities. I think, if I go forward in a planned way, I will be able to do the best. A Politician is a person engaged or interested in politics and politics is a science, and art of government, political affairs or life or principles. Politicians play a central role in our lives. They are the persons who make all efforts to improve their constituencies' and people's welfare. Good and dependable politicians are always delighted to serve the people and consider themselves as servants of the people or the state. The represent the hopes, aspirations, and the interests of very citizen in the statue. So I am of the view that, if I become a politician, I would be able to serve the country well. Many people hardly think of becoming a politician and lead their career as politicians. This is because of negative image of politicians in doing their duties. The political situation in most of the developing countries is not passing through a good condition. But I think, this situation will not continue more. Many good citizens are joining the politics. When I will grow up as a good citizen, I will take the helm of a political party of the country and make the politics favourable to people. I know a reliable politician should be very loyal and committed to his party and the people of the country. A good politician should be honest, well disciplined personality with selfless service, brilliant, skilful and patriotic. I think I have these qualities and I will nurture such kinds of qualities and values so that I can be a dynamic one as a future leader of the country. A good politician must be a good social worker. As a politician, I will have the opportunity to work for people, solve their problems and do development work for the rapid progress of the country. I will be on the side of the people when my party come of power. I will dispels all negative conceptions attributed to the politicians. I already have mentioned the fact the politics is the great way to serve the nation. This is the main reason to be a politician. I always cherish the desire to help the distressed people in solving their poverty, illiteracy. I will upgrade the living standard of our people. I will free the country from corruption, malpractice of power and social discrimination. When my party come to power, I will be a minister and do my best to serve the country and always be on the side of the poor people irrespective of cast, creed and religion. In conclusion, I may say that a politician is a respectable and powerful person in the society who is mostly known to all.As a politician, I must have ample opportunity to serve the nation and be remain on the people. I will share in their joys and sorrows and lead the nation towards the way of progress.Thus my country Bangladesh will be able to pose herself as a developed and glorious country in the map of the world. So I think, my aim to be a politician is a perfect choice.. Physical Exercise Physical Exercise is any bodily activity that enhances or maintains physical fitness and overall health and wellness. It is performed for various reasons including strengthening muscles and the cardiovascular systems, honing athletic skills, weight loss or maintenance, as well as the purpose of enjoyment. There are many kinds of physical exercises . They are of two classes, such as body building exercise and outdoor games. A body building exercise can be done inside a room. But the outdoor games are played in an open field. The outdoor games are generally football, cricket, volleyball, badminton, hockey, tennis and so on. Swimming, running, cycling and long walking are also very good kind of physical excises. Morning and evening are the best time for physical exercise. It should be done regularly and punctually. It should not be done more than one hour at a time. Because of having bad effect of over exercise, we should do what actually permits our body according to the suggestion of medical science. We should not eat or drink and talk or laugh while taking physical exercise. Exercise should be done in a neat, clean and open place with full of light and fresh air. Constant and regular exercise is imperative for the preservation of good health, otherwise the mind is distracted by bodily pain and weakness. It makes our body strong, fit and active. Our limbs become stronger. Besides, our heart and lungs become stronger. During exercise the whole body perspires and dirt beneath the skin is cleaned away. After all, for doing various kinds of physical exercise, the blood circulation works well and thus we remain free from any disease. Physical exercise is an abiding source of pleasure. Our  mind and soul remain dull, if we don't follow any kind of exercise. It refreshes our mind and keeps our temperament cool and fresh. Regular physical exercise inspires us to do our duties with a fresh mood. We may draw a conclusion saying that physical exercise is good for health and an abiding source of pleasure. It helps to keep our body fit and fresh and prevents heart attack, diabetes, obesity and many other diseases. It also improves our mental strength and removes depression. We should be punctual of doing physical exercise on regular basis. A News paper/ Importance of Reading Newspapers A news paper is a powerful means of communication in today's world. It is a very important organ of the world voice of our present time. In the modern democratic world, it is widely known as the Fourth Estate. All types of newspapers of the world keep the people updated giving instant news and views of home and abroad. The News paper contains the news from every corner of the world. The news papers are of different kinds. They are dailies, weeklies, monthlies, magazines etc. In Bangladesh, we have some established and standard newspaper such as the Daily star, The Bangladesh Observer, The Daily Itefaq, The Prothom Alo, The jugantar, The Morning Sun etc. Some newspapers have online edition that publish news instantly. The news paper is a mirror of the world. It shows us each morning all that have happened in the last twenty four hours.. It is a record of the life of every nation and all the important national and international issues. Thus the newspaper keeps us well informed about happenings in every corner of the world, because a news paper contains news, views, opinions, advertisements , notices  etc. It also includes update of share market, sports news, news about film and drama. The most important role of the newspaper is to influence the people to be united to fight against crime, corruption, injustice, irregularities of the government and any other institution. The government pays attention to the news and can take immediate action regarding this. In case of public grievances, the government sincerely tries to redress them. Thus newspaper is regarded as mirror of public views. The Newspaper provides solution to various social and economic and political problem by publishing articles on various issues. In a democratic country, the newspaper plays the role of the guardian of the rights of the people. It also serves as a link between the government and the people. The newspaper helps the students and the job seekers. It publishes   educational pages with the content of different subjects. The job seekers can get appointment notice and advertisement of government and non government institution. They also get appointment information, news of written and final test's result through newspapers. So the newspaper has educative value. In conclusion, we may say that the news paper plays an important role in the field of education, entertainment, politics, economic and democracy. But it should be free from the intervention of government. It should be fair in its reporting. The newspaper should have a very strict code of ethics to which it should conscientiously adhere to. The game you like most/ your favorite game Games and sports are always interesting and thrilling. It is the best medium of recreation and pleasure. Games give a man life and inspiration to lead a life joyfully. I am always fond of games and sports. Among all the games that give me much pleasure, I like football most. The reason behind the choice are immense. Football is my best choice. It is the most popular sport in world. It is well known as king sport. It has been my favourite sport since my childhood. I feel excited when I watch any football match on TV or in the stadium. It gives me much pleasure and draws my attention to it. Football is a wonderful game which is played between two teams each consisting of eleven players. In the field, there is a referee and two line men assisting the referee. The objective of the game is to score by kicking the ball into the opposition goal post. The winner is the team which eventually score more goals at the end of the match. Football is a popular game all over the world. There is no place in the world where football is not played. It has become the most popular game in the world. We realize this truth when we see all the nations around the world become excited with the FIFA world cup football. They spend many nights sleepless and keep their eyes on the television screen. I am one of the football lovers as it is my favourite game. Football is one of the most fascinating games from the point of view of the spectators and its significance. Football has a pleasing touch by which it may unite all the nations in a queue. It has such a power that reduces conflicts among the nations. Football is that power which can make a bridge between the nations of the world . In conclusion, we say that football is the most powerful and important game in the world. I like it most because of its great significance in various aspects. It is a good exercise. It helps me to make new friends. The sport teaches its players to be disciplined. It raises the spirit of love and co-operation among the players. It can enhance relation among the nations of the world. So I can't think other than football. Population Problem  of Bangladesh Population problem is one of the most talked about topics of the world now a days. In Bangladesh, it has been declared as number one national problem. Hence Bangladesh is called an over populated country. In fact, her present population has come to a stage of explosion. The population of Bangladesh has become a great problem. The present growth rate of population is 1.147 percent. rcent. This rate is very alarming and disturbing. If this growth rate continues, her population will be double by the end of 25th century. Now about 16 core people live in Bangladesh, a country of 147570 square km making Bangladesh one of the ten most populous countries in the world. The population of Bangladesh is increasing by geometrical progressing while agricultural production is increasing by arithmetical progression. The population becomes problem if the overall density of population is much higher than its territory, property and ability to feed them. If we think about it in deeper level, we can find many causes of population problem. These causes are discussed in detail below. Illiteracy is the most important cause of population problem in Bangladesh. The illiterate people are not aware of their responsibilities and they cannot realize the importance of family planning. So they become parents of so many poor children. Religious bigotry is another cause of over population in the country. Some orthodox people opine that to adopt family planning measure is a sinful act. As a result, population goes on increasing day by day. In Bangladesh death rate is lower than birth rate that contributes to increase new faces every year. The system of polygamy has worsened the population problem in the country. The custom of early marriage is still prevalent in the rural areas. It is the prime cause of increasing population at an alarming rate. 50% of the total population of our country are of the female sex. Most of them are illiterate. They are not aware of their next generation. They don't follow family planning. So they give birth to many children every year. Over population creates a lot of problems. In addition, this problem acts as a national problem of the country. We see several adverse impacts on the society for over population. A poor country like Bangladesh, having a huge population, cannot provide the proper midicare. Over population creates unemployment problem. An increase in the population has a direct impact on the unrest and various forms of crimes. An increase in population hampers education, medical facilities and other basic needs of people as government is not able to ease these facilities for everyone. The problem of overpopulation should be properly addressed. So the following measures should be taken to control the problem- To educate the masses. To adopt family planning. To award parents having only two children. Creating awareness among the unconscious people about the adverse effect of over population. Stopping early marriage. Facilitate female education and erecting employment opportunities. In conclusion,it can be said that the growth rate of Bangladesh is alarming. The impact of over population is so grievous and pathetic that there is no way other than solving the problem. On the other hand, we should find out the ways and means to utilize the existing population so that they can be manpower instead of being problem for the country. Science in everyday life Science plays a vital role in our daily lives. It is all around us and has become an inseparable part of modern human life. It has made our lives easier, faster, and more comfortable. From the moment we wake up to the time we go to sleep, we are surrounded by the wonders of science. One of the most important contributions of science is in the field of communication. Mobile phones, the internet, and television are all gifts of science. They help us stay connected with the world and access information within seconds. For example, we can talk to someone far away through a video call or send a message instantly. Social media, email, and online news are all possible due to scientific inventions. Similarly, science has made our household tasks much easier. Appliances like refrigerators, washing machines, microwave ovens, vacuum cleaners, and electric irons save both time and effort. These machines help us complete chores quickly and comfortably, which gives us more time for rest and relaxation. In the field of transportation, science has brought revolutionary changes. In the past, people had to travel long distances on foot or by animal carts, which took days or even weeks. But today, we have cars, buses, trains, and airplanes that can carry us to faraway places in just a few hours. These inventions save time and make travel more convenient. Science has also made great progress in the field of medicine. Many diseases that were once deadly can now be cured, thanks to modern treatments and vaccines. Hospitals use scientific machines like X-ray, ECG, and MRI scanners to detect diseases early and treat patients properly. Because of medical science, people are now living longer and healthier lives. Moreover, science helps in agriculture too. Farmers use modern tools, chemical fertilizers, and scientific methods to grow more food in less time. Machines like tractors and harvesters have replaced hard manual labor. As a result, we have more food to feed the growing population. Science also plays a big role in education. Students now use computers, tablets, and projectors in classrooms. Online learning has made it possible to attend classes from anywhere in the world. Science has made education more interactive and enjoyable. Even in entertainment, science has had a strong impact. We can watch movies, listen to music, play video games, or browse the internet at any time. All these forms of entertainment are made possible through modern technology. In conclusion, science is a blessing in our daily lives. It has touched every aspect of human life and continues to improve it every day. Without science, our lives would be slow, hard, and uncomfortable. However, we must also be careful to use science wisely and responsibly. If used properly, science can continue to make the world a better place for everyone.

Duties of a student A student is a person who devotes himself to the pursuit of knowledge and learning. Today's Students are the future citizen of the country. They can only take the challenge of the coming days, if the students are sincere and they acquire the education in real sense. It is therefore, the first and foremost duty of a student is to seek knowledge and prepare himself with good qualities that a student should acquire. The students constitute a great potential force capable of leading a nation to the greatest heights of progress. So, the future of every nation depends much on their students. The students should consider this fact with sincere and they should improve their intellect and widen their mental capabilities through acquiring knowledge. A student should be punctual in attending classes, where he learns the good traits of life such as, discipline, obedience, dutifulness, diligences, respect to elders, honesty, patriotism and other qualities to lead the nation towards development, peace and prosperity. It is the duty of every student to acquire these fine qualities of life which will enable him/her to stand in good stead in the future. Another important duty of a student is to prepare himself for the career he wants to pursue. It is in school life that the future career takes roots. During school life, one becomes aware of one's abilities, limitations and different professions available. Therefore, during this stage he should make up his mind as to what he should become in future. A student has a duty to fight against all evils existing in the society. He has a tremendous responsibility to stand against injustice, inequality, oppression, exploitation, corruption, criminalization of politics, communalism, casteism, misappropriation of public money etc. The students also have a great responsibilities to help the less fortunate members of the society by way of social work. They can offer great services by teaching illiterate children of the society. The students should devote themselves in creating public awareness on various social and political issues. A student can also render valuable services to the nation at the time of calamities such as, floods, earthquakes, severe drought and accidents. The students should be ready to perform valuable services to the cause of the nation. They should stand by the country at the time of national emergencies. A good student should take part in games and sports and other extra-curricular activities to make himself fully prepared to face the challenges in the years to come. In this regard, a student should be punctual, hard working and dutiful and a man with moral character and high vision. In conclusion, we should focus on the fact that the prime and foremost duty of a student is to seek knowledge and wisdom and he must gladly submit to the rules of discipline to be a good citizen of the country. He should be symbol of love and a real patriot. If the students are not aware of the essential qualities that a student should acquire at the right time, the country cannot expect much  from a student. The students should remember it and perform their duties accordingly. Discipline Discipline is a method of training of one's mind or body or of controlling one's behavior. It also refers to order or code of conduct. It is a systematic method of obtaining obedience. It is absolutely necessary for peace, progress, prosperity of an individual as well as the society. It is a process and controlled behavior that strengthens law and order in the society. Discipline has its own value and influence of all spheres of life. Life without self discipline is no life. As it is a set/rules, we need to be guided by rules and regulations. Life without discipline is bound to go astray and is totally meaningless. With proper discipline, an individual or a country can be led in a right track. Where there is no discipline, there is no progress and no success. Discipline brings peace, progress and tranquility in our life and country. If we want to keep our country free from political turmoil and social unrest, every citizens must obey the rules and laws of the country. It must be observed and maintained in the political offairs and activities. Otherwise, political conflict and the conflict of interest may exist in the political process that may lead the whole nation in an untoward situation. There is value of discipline in all walks of life. Whether at school or at home, in the office or in the factory, in play ground or in the battlefield discipline is a must. Without discipline, there must have complete chaos, unrest and disorder all the while. There will be no peace and stability if discipline is not maintained. For example, in school if the boys are not disciplined, the boys themselves will learn nothing and the very purpose of education will be incompleted. Similarly, if the subordinates do not obey their senior officers in offices and factories, it will become difficult to run the administrative activities and there will be no production there. Discipline in the army and the law and order agencies i.. very remarkable. If the soldiers don't carry out the orders of their commanders and generals, the whole system within its structure will be collapsed and they will not be established as an effective force. The police is the members of law and order agencies. If the police refuses to carry out their duties and responsibilities, and if they are not led by their organizational rules and regulations, the law and order situation of the country will be disrupted and public life will be abnormal. Nature shows a shining example of discipline. The sun, the moon and all the planets of the universe follow discipline strictly. We also see the living example a of discipline in ants, bees and other living being of our environment. In conclusion, we may say that discipline is the secret of success. Life and society without discipline are absolutely meaningless. Without discipline, life will be conflicting, hazardous and ineffective. So discipline must be maintained everywhere. The higher is the sense of disciple, the better it is the people and the country. Tree Plantation Tree plantation is a process of planting trees-seedling generally for conservation of forest and land scalping   purposes. It is a key issue at this moment all over the world. Trees are one of the main elements working to save our environment. They are closely related to our life as well as the other animals. Plantation of trees is thus a crying need of the time. Trees are the best gift of nature. Nature decorates itself with trees showing eye-catering beauty every where in our planet. Trios expose not only beauty but also put immense benefit for human being and other living being in the world. So planting of trees has been given priority to increase the beauty of nature and put the environment in a better position.. Since the dawn of civilization, there were innumerable trees all over the world. But with the passage of time, men started cutting of trees indiscriminately exposing a threat to our environment. Now it is a global concern. People all over world are eager to plant more trees and like go with the campaign. "Grow more trees and save the environment". With the absence of required and sufficient trees, the world's temperature is getting warmer gradually., This situation has changed our climate and eco-system tremendously and caused green house effect. Only plantation of more trees can prevent the environment from pollution. Without oxygen we cannot live for a moment that we get from the tree. Trees take in carbon dioxide and produce oxygen. Carbon dioxide makes our atmosphere polluted. Trees consume this cardon dioxide and thereby makes our environment safe for us. Other than the aforesaid issues, trees came to our life in many ways. We get fruits, flowers, fire woods and timbers. Fruit bearing trees provide us with many types of fruits for our good health. They give us shade and reduce our tiredness. They are used as timber for making ey-catching furniture. Tree plantation draws attention of the people to the fact of environmental degradation all over the world. Due to lack of required forest land, the world experiences less rainfall, draught resulting desertification. The low-lying countries of the world like Bangladesh, Maldives, Sreelanka, have to fight against natural disasters like sidre, Ila, Nargis and other cyclones and floods. Thanks to atmospheric pollution, we should plant trees near our houses, villages and in the forest land for forestation with a view to creating new forest. We should also plant trees on the both sides of the roads. We should advise all to plant two trees, if they cut a sigle one. Generally, rainy season is the best time for tree plantation. Most of the tree plants thrive well in rainy season. But only planting the tree is not enough. Because, planted trees may be destroyed by animals or otherwise. So we should be very careful about the newly planted trees. In conclusion, We may say that the government and the private organizations. Should raise awareness of the people for tree plantation to address all the issues related to our existence. Besides, attempts should be made and environmental laws should be implemented and new law should be enacted to stop cutting trees so that we may get a polluted free environment and live in a better world. My Hobby/ Favourite Hobby/Traveling as a Hobby. Hobby involves activities that we do for pleasure during our free time. It brings joy happiness, and refreshes our mind and soul together. It is the interest of a person in anything other than his main career or pre -occupation. As a Student, my main duty is to acquire knowledge and wisdom. But I believe only educational institution cannot satisfy my thirst for knowledge and cannot give me much pleasure. So I like to do something extra. I have keen interest in traveling when I am out of academic activities. Hobby differs from man to man according to his sweet choice. There are many kinds of hobby such as gardening, stamp collecting, hiking, coin-collecting, photography, listening to music, writing poems and novels, drawing picture, traveling, reading and so forth. Among the aforesaid hobbies, I like traveling as it gives me much pleasure. It is my ultimate choice as a hobby. Other people think that traveling is an expensive hobby and time consuming one. But I am of the view regarding choice, though it costs much money, its intrinsic value compensates the financial loss. So I think I have the right choice. As I have no other choice but traveling as a hobby, I make a pleasant tour in and outside my country. When I get-time during dimmer vacation, winter vacation or after the final examination is over, I make long or short trip to home and abroad. The world is so big and there are so many places to visit. Each has a different beauty, various summer, culture, language, history and customs. The hobby of travelling is a hobby of enduring the beauty of Allah's creativity and skillfulness. I like to get the opportunity given by Allah by travelling everywhere whenever I can. Regarding travelling place, my first choice is to visit my country's most popular and historical places. Cox's Bazar, Kuakata, Rangamati, Paherpur, Maynamati etc are of my first priority. But when I get long vacation, I make special tour to foreign country's noted tourist spots with my family members. There is no doubt that a person gets more life experience by walking a mile than reading an entire book. By traveling, we can make new friends, get rid of day to day frustration, and get something new. That is to sayit gives me a great break from my routine and an opportunity to learn a new culture, old history and make life long friends. It opens up a new avenue to take pleasure in learning the other cultures and languages. This type of traveling opens up my mind from a particular bend of thinking. In conclusion, I may say that traveling, my favourite hobby is an excellent way to see new places and to get new knowledge, experiences about new culture. I am of the view that it is a perfect educational hobby as it helps me learn a lot of new things in life. It is both educative and entertaining. So I think I have the right choice in selecting favourite hobby and it is definitely traveling. Your Aim in Life, or, Your future Plan Aim in life is a series of events and feelings that happen in our mind when we grow up. It may come to us in any stage of our life. Future plan is that way which enables us to work in a planned way to reach the goal. Different people have different aims and plans. So I have a high ambition in life. The aim which I cherish in my mind is to serve the country and this my ultimate aim in life. To materialize the aim, I have decided to become a politician and social worker. Thanks to my great desire to serve the people, I need to educate myself for well preparation to make the plan a success. That is way I want to get the higher education from a famous educational institution in my country. I am fond of reading and writing. I will work hard to achieve knowledge and wisdom that I will apply in my practical life. I will devote myself to bring good result in my educational life. For this purpose, I do study regularly and participate in extra-curricular activities. I think, if I go forward in a planned way, I will be able to do the best. A Politician is a person engaged or interested in politics and politics is a science, and art of government, political affairs or life or principles. Politicians play a central role in our lives. They are the persons who make all efforts to improve their constituencies' and people's welfare. Good and dependable politicians are always delighted to serve the people and consider themselves as servants of the people or the state. The represent the hopes, aspirations, and the interests of very citizen in the statue. So I am of the view that, if I become a politician, I would be able to serve the country well. Many people hardly think of becoming a politician and lead their career as politicians. This is because of negative image of politicians in doing their duties. The political situation in most of the developing countries is not passing through a good condition. But I think, this situation will not continue more. Many good citizens are joining the politics. When I will grow up as a good citizen, I will take the helm of a political party of the country and make the politics favourable to people. I know a reliable politician should be very loyal and committed to his party and the people of the country. A good politician should be honest, well disciplined personality with selfless service, brilliant, skilful and patriotic. I think I have these qualities and I will nurture such kinds of qualities and values so that I can be a dynamic one as a future leader of the country. A good politician must be a good social worker. As a politician, I will have the opportunity to work for people, solve their problems and do development work for the rapid progress of the country. I will be on the side of the people when my party come of power. I will dispels all negative conceptions attributed to the politicians. I already have mentioned the fact the politics is the great way to serve the nation. This is the main reason to be a politician. I always cherish the desire to help the distressed people in solving their poverty, illiteracy. I will upgrade the living standard of our people. I will free the country from corruption, malpractice of power and social discrimination. When my party come to power, I will be a minister and do my best to serve the country and always be on the side of the poor people irrespective of cast, creed and religion. In conclusion, I may say that a politician is a respectable and powerful person in the society who is mostly known to all.As a politician, I must have ample opportunity to serve the nation and be remain on the people. I will share in their joys and sorrows and lead the nation towards the way of progress.Thus my country Bangladesh will be able to pose herself as a developed and glorious country in the map of the world. So I think, my aim to be a politician is a perfect choice.. Physical Exercise Physical Exercise is any bodily activity that enhances or maintains physical fitness and overall health and wellness. It is performed for various reasons including strengthening muscles and the cardiovascular systems, honing athletic skills, weight loss or maintenance, as well as the purpose of enjoyment. There are many kinds of physical exercises . They are of two classes, such as body building exercise and outdoor games. A body building exercise can be done inside a room. But the outdoor games are played in an open field. The outdoor games are generally football, cricket, volleyball, badminton, hockey, tennis and so on. Swimming, running, cycling and long walking are also very good kind of physical excises. Morning and evening are the best time for physical exercise. It should be done regularly and punctually. It should not be done more than one hour at a time. Because of having bad effect of over exercise, we should do what actually permits our body according to the suggestion of medical science. We should not eat or drink and talk or laugh while taking physical exercise. Exercise should be done in a neat, clean and open place with full of light and fresh air. Constant and regular exercise is imperative for the preservation of good health, otherwise the mind is distracted by bodily pain and weakness. It makes our body strong, fit and active. Our limbs become stronger. Besides, our heart and lungs become stronger. During exercise the whole body perspires and dirt beneath the skin is cleaned away. After all, for doing various kinds of physical exercise, the blood circulation works well and thus we remain free from any disease. Physical exercise is an abiding source of pleasure. Our  mind and soul remain dull, if we don't follow any kind of exercise. It refreshes our mind and keeps our temperament cool and fresh. Regular physical exercise inspires us to do our duties with a fresh mood. We may draw a conclusion saying that physical exercise is good for health and an abiding source of pleasure. It helps to keep our body fit and fresh and prevents heart attack, diabetes, obesity and many other diseases. It also improves our mental strength and removes depression. We should be punctual of doing physical exercise on regular basis. A News paper/ Importance of Reading Newspapers A news paper is a powerful means of communication in today's world. It is a very important organ of the world voice of our present time. In the modern democratic world, it is widely known as the Fourth Estate. All types of newspapers of the world keep the people updated giving instant news and views of home and abroad. The News paper contains the news from every corner of the world. The news papers are of different kinds. They are dailies, weeklies, monthlies, magazines etc. In Bangladesh, we have some established and standard newspaper such as the Daily star, The Bangladesh Observer, The Daily Itefaq, The Prothom Alo, The jugantar, The Morning Sun etc. Some newspapers have online edition that publish news instantly. The news paper is a mirror of the world. It shows us each morning all that have happened in the last twenty four hours.. It is a record of the life of every nation and all the important national and international issues. Thus the newspaper keeps us well informed about happenings in every corner of the world, because a news paper contains news, views, opinions, advertisements , notices  etc. It also includes update of share market, sports news, news about film and drama. The most important role of the newspaper is to influence the people to be united to fight against crime, corruption, injustice, irregularities of the government and any other institution. The government pays attention to the news and can take immediate action regarding this. In case of public grievances, the government sincerely tries to redress them. Thus newspaper is regarded as mirror of public views. The Newspaper provides solution to various social and economic and political problem by publishing articles on various issues. In a democratic country, the newspaper plays the role of the guardian of the rights of the people. It also serves as a link between the government and the people. The newspaper helps the students and the job seekers. It publishes   educational pages with the content of different subjects. The job seekers can get appointment notice and advertisement of government and non government institution. They also get appointment information, news of written and final test's result through newspapers. So the newspaper has educative value. In conclusion, we may say that the news paper plays an important role in the field of education, entertainment, politics, economic and democracy. But it should be free from the intervention of government. It should be fair in its reporting. The newspaper should have a very strict code of ethics to which it should conscientiously adhere to. The game you like most/ your favorite game Games and sports are always interesting and thrilling. It is the best medium of recreation and pleasure. Games give a man life and inspiration to lead a life joyfully. I am always fond of games and sports. Among all the games that give me much pleasure, I like football most. The reason behind the choice are immense. Football is my best choice. It is the most popular sport in world. It is well known as king sport. It has been my favourite sport since my childhood. I feel excited when I watch any football match on TV or in the stadium. It gives me much pleasure and draws my attention to it. Football is a wonderful game which is played between two teams each consisting of eleven players. In the field, there is a referee and two line men assisting the referee. The objective of the game is to score by kicking the ball into the opposition goal post. The winner is the team which eventually score more goals at the end of the match. Football is a popular game all over the world. There is no place in the world where football is not played. It has become the most popular game in the world. We realize this truth when we see all the nations around the world become excited with the FIFA world cup football. They spend many nights sleepless and keep their eyes on the television screen. I am one of the football lovers as it is my favourite game. Football is one of the most fascinating games from the point of view of the spectators and its significance. Football has a pleasing touch by which it may unite all the nations in a queue. It has such a power that reduces conflicts among the nations. Football is that power which can make a bridge between the nations of the world . In conclusion, we say that football is the most powerful and important game in the world. I like it most because of its great significance in various aspects. It is a good exercise. It helps me to make new friends. The sport teaches its players to be disciplined. It raises the spirit of love and co-operation among the players. It can enhance relation among the nations of the world. So I can't think other than football. Population Problem  of Bangladesh Population problem is one of the most talked about topics of the world now a days. In Bangladesh, it has been declared as number one national problem. Hence Bangladesh is called an over populated country. In fact, her present population has come to a stage of explosion. The population of Bangladesh has become a great problem. The present growth rate of population is 1.147 percent. rcent. This rate is very alarming and disturbing. If this growth rate continues, her population will be double by the end of 25th century. Now about 16 core people live in Bangladesh, a country of 147570 square km making Bangladesh one of the ten most populous countries in the world. The population of Bangladesh is increasing by geometrical progressing while agricultural production is increasing by arithmetical progression. The population becomes problem if the overall density of population is much higher than its territory, property and ability to feed them. If we think about it in deeper level, we can find many causes of population problem. These causes are discussed in detail below. Illiteracy is the most important cause of population problem in Bangladesh. The illiterate people are not aware of their responsibilities and they cannot realize the importance of family planning. So they become parents of so many poor children. Religious bigotry is another cause of over population in the country. Some orthodox people opine that to adopt family planning measure is a sinful act. As a result, population goes on increasing day by day. In Bangladesh death rate is lower than birth rate that contributes to increase new faces every year. The system of polygamy has worsened the population problem in the country. The custom of early marriage is still prevalent in the rural areas. It is the prime cause of increasing population at an alarming rate. 50% of the total population of our country are of the female sex. Most of them are illiterate. They are not aware of their next generation. They don't follow family planning. So they give birth to many children every year. Over population creates a lot of problems. In addition, this problem acts as a national problem of the country. We see several adverse impacts on the society for over population. A poor country like Bangladesh, having a huge population, cannot provide the proper midicare. Over population creates unemployment problem. An increase in the population has a direct impact on the unrest and various forms of crimes. An increase in population hampers education, medical facilities and other basic needs of people as government is not able to ease these facilities for everyone. The problem of overpopulation should be properly addressed. So the following measures should be taken to control the problem- To educate the masses. To adopt family planning. To award parents having only two children. Creating awareness among the unconscious people about the adverse effect of over population. Stopping early marriage. Facilitate female education and erecting employment opportunities. In conclusion,it can be said that the growth rate of Bangladesh is alarming. The impact of over population is so grievous and pathetic that there is no way other than solving the problem. On the other hand, we should find out the ways and means to utilize the existing population so that they can be manpower instead of being problem for the country. Science in everyday life Science plays a vital role in our daily lives. It is all around us and has become an inseparable part of modern human life. It has made our lives easier, faster, and more comfortable. From the moment we wake up to the time we go to sleep, we are surrounded by the wonders of science. One of the most important contributions of science is in the field of communication. Mobile phones, the internet, and television are all gifts of science. They help us stay connected with the world and access information within seconds. For example, we can talk to someone far away through a video call or send a message instantly. Social media, email, and online news are all possible due to scientific inventions. Similarly, science has made our household tasks much easier. Appliances like refrigerators, washing machines, microwave ovens, vacuum cleaners, and electric irons save both time and effort. These machines help us complete chores quickly and comfortably, which gives us more time for rest and relaxation. In the field of transportation, science has brought revolutionary changes. In the past, people had to travel long distances on foot or by animal carts, which took days or even weeks. But today, we have cars, buses, trains, and airplanes that can carry us to faraway places in just a few hours. These inventions save time and make travel more convenient. Science has also made great progress in the field of medicine. Many diseases that were once deadly can now be cured, thanks to modern treatments and vaccines. Hospitals use scientific machines like X-ray, ECG, and MRI scanners to detect diseases early and treat patients properly. Because of medical science, people are now living longer and healthier lives. Moreover, science helps in agriculture too. Farmers use modern tools, chemical fertilizers, and scientific methods to grow more food in less time. Machines like tractors and harvesters have replaced hard manual labor. As a result, we have more food to feed the growing population. Science also plays a big role in education. Students now use computers, tablets, and projectors in classrooms. Online learning has made it possible to attend classes from anywhere in the world. Science has made education more interactive and enjoyable. Even in entertainment, science has had a strong impact. We can watch movies, listen to music, play video games, or browse the internet at any time. All these forms of entertainment are made possible through modern technology. In conclusion, science is a blessing in our daily lives. It has touched every aspect of human life and continues to improve it every day. Without science, our lives would be slow, hard, and uncomfortable. However, we must also be careful to use science wisely and responsibly. If used properly, science can continue to make the world a better place for everyone.

text: 1. The Pied Piper of Hamelin The town of Hamelin in Germany was once troubled by a severe rat infestation. The rats were everywhere—inside homes, in the streets, and even in food stores. The people were desperate and didn’t know how to get rid of them. One day, a strange man wearing a colorful coat came to the town. He called himself the Pied Piper and offered to remove all the rats in exchange for a thousand gold coins. The mayor and townspeople agreed. The Pied Piper took out a magical pipe and played a special tune. To everyone’s surprise, the rats came out from every corner and followed him. He led them to the River Weser, where they drowned. After the rats were gone, the Piper returned to collect his reward. However, the mayor broke his promise and refused to pay the full amount. Angry and disappointed, the Piper left. The next day, the Piper came back and played another tune—this time, a sweet and cheerful one. All the children of Hamelin followed him, dancing and laughing. He led them into a mountain, which magically closed behind them. Only one child, who was lame and couldn't keep up, remained behind. The people of Hamelin were heartbroken. They had lost all their children because of their dishonesty. Since then, the story has served as a warning to always keep one’s promises. Moral: We should always keep our promises and act honestly.

text: 2. A story on honesty  is the best policy. Honesty is the Best Policy Once upon a time, there lived a poor woodcutter named Atiq. He was an honest and hardworking man. Every day, Atiq would go deep into the forest to cut wood. He sold the wood in the nearby market to earn money for his family. Although he was poor, he never lied or cheated anyone. One sunny morning, Atiq went to the forest as usual. While cutting wood near a river, his axe suddenly slipped from his hand and fell into the deep water. The river was very wide and full of strong currents. Atiq looked into the water, but he could not see his axe. He became very upset, as it was the only tool he had to earn a living. Atiq sat by the riverbank and started to pray for help. Just then, a beautiful fairy appeared from the river. She asked, “Why are you so sad, kind man?” Atiq stood up and replied, “Dear fairy, my only axe has fallen into the river. Without it, I cannot cut wood or feed my family.” The fairy smiled and said, “Don’t worry. I will help you.” She dived into the river and soon came out with a golden axe. She asked Atiq, “Is this your axe?” Atiq looked at it and said honestly, “No, this is not mine. My axe was not made of gold.” The fairy went into the water again and returned with a silver axe. She asked again, “Is this one yours?” Atiq shook his head and replied, “No, this is also not mine.” Finally, the fairy brought out an iron axe. Atiq’s face lit up with joy. “Yes!” he said, “This is my axe!” The fairy was very pleased with Atiq’s honesty. She said, “You are a good and honest man. As a reward for your truthfulness, I will give you all three axes—the iron one, the silver one, and the golden one.” Atiq was surprised and thanked the fairy with a grateful heart. From that day on, Atiq became well-known in his village for his honesty. He continued to live a simple life, but he was never poor again. Everyone respected him and learned from his example. Moral: Honesty is always rewarded.

text: 3. The  consequences of greed The price of greed Once upon a time, a poor farmer lived in a small village with his wife. One day, he discovered something unusual in the nest of his goose—a shining, solid golden egg. At first, he thought it was a trick, but when it happened again the next day, he realized that the goose was truly special. Every morning, the goose laid one golden egg. The farmer and his wife sold the eggs and slowly became very rich. They built a bigger house, bought new clothes, and lived a comfortable life. However, as their wealth increased, so did their greed. They were no longer satisfied with one egg a day. The wife said to her husband, “Why should we wait every day for one egg? The goose must have a lot of gold inside her. Let’s cut her open and take it all at once.” Blinded by greed, the farmer agreed. They killed the goose, hoping to find a great treasure inside. But to their shock, there was no gold inside—just like any ordinary goose. Their source of wealth was gone forever. The couple regretted their foolish decision, but it was too late. Their greed had cost them everything. They soon became poor again and lived in sorrow for the rest of their lives. Moral:  Greed always leads to destruction.

text: 4. A story on where there is a will  there is a way or Failures are the pillars of success Many years ago, there lived a king of Scotland named Robert Bruce. He was a brave and patriotic ruler. He loved his country deeply and wanted to free it from the hands of the English. He fought many battles to gain independence for his people. But unfortunately, he was defeated six times in the battlefield. Each time, his soldiers lost courage and deserted him. Heartbroken and hopeless, the king hid himself in a lonely cave. One rainy day, lying on the floor of the cave, the king was lost in deep thought. Suddenly, he noticed a little spider trying to climb up the roof of the cave. The spider tried once but fell down. It tried again and again, but failed each time. Yet it did not lose heart. At last, on its seventh attempt, the spider succeeded in climbing up to the roof. This small incident gave the king a great lesson. He thought, “If a tiny spider can succeed after repeated failures, why should I, a man and a king, lose courage?” His heart filled with new hope and strength. He came out of the cave, gathered his soldiers again, and inspired them with fresh determination. They fought bravely under his leadership. This time, fortune favored him. King Bruce defeated the English army and regained the freedom of his country. The story of King Bruce and the spider teaches us a valuable lesson. Failure is not the end of life. Every failure gives us an opportunity to correct our mistakes and prepare better for the next attempt. Success comes only to those who keep trying with patience and courage. Just as King Bruce learned from the spider, we too must remember that failure is the pillar of success. Moral :  “Perseverance and patience lead to victory.

text: 5. A story on rates and the monkey or  dividing the bread The Two Rats and the Monkey Once upon a time, two hungry cats lived in a village. One day, they found a piece of bread on the roadside. Both cats jumped at it at the same time and caught hold of it. Each claimed that the bread belonged to him. They began to quarrel loudly. One cat said, “I found it first, so it is mine.” The other argued, “But I touched it first, so it belongs to me.” As they could not settle the matter, they thought of dividing the bread equally. But neither trusted the other to do the division fairly. While they were still quarreling, a clever monkey happened to pass by. The monkey was very cunning and greedy. He thought to himself, “This is a good chance for me to have a nice meal. I will trick these foolish cats.” The monkey came forward and said, “My dear friends, why are you quarreling? Let me help you. I am wise and fair, so I will divide the bread equally.” The cats, being simple, agreed and handed the bread to him. The monkey tore the bread into two pieces, but one piece was slightly larger than the other. He frowned and said, “Oh, this is not fair. Let me take a bite from the bigger piece to make them equal.” Saying this, he ate a portion of the larger piece. But now the other piece looked bigger. So the monkey took a bite from it too. He continued biting alternately from both pieces, pretending to make them equal. Each time he ate, he shook his head and said, “Still not equal. Let me try again.” In this way, the monkey went on eating until almost the whole bread was finished. Finally, only two small crumbs were left. The monkey gave one crumb to each cat and said, “Now the bread is divided equally. Farewell, my friends.” The two cats looked at each other in despair. They were both still hungry and realized they had been fooled by the clever monkey. They had lost their bread because of their foolish quarrel and lack of trust. From that day on, the cats learned a bitter lesson: selfish quarrels only invite others to take advantage, and unity and fairness are always better than fighting over small gains. Moral: When we quarrel among ourselves, others take advantage.

text: 6. A story on nobody believes  a  liar The Liar Cowboy / Shepherd Once there was a cowboy named Arif who lived on the outskirts of a small village. He was well-known among the villagers, but not for a good reason. Arif loved to tell lies and exaggerate every small thing that happened to him. One day he rode into the village shouting, “Help! Wild wolves are attacking my cattle!” Hearing this, the villagers ran to help him with sticks and tools. But when they reached his farm, they found everything safe. Arif laughed and said, “I just wanted to see how fast you could run!” The villagers were angry but forgave him. A few weeks later, Arif shouted again, “Fire! My barn is burning!” Once more, the villagers ran to help, only to find it was another lie. Arif laughed again, thinking it was fun to trick everyone. Slowly, people stopped believing him. One evening, while Arif was out on the plain, a real danger appeared. A pack of wolves came suddenly and attacked his herd. Arif ran into the village shouting, “Help! Wolves are really attacking now!” But the villagers, remembering all his lies, ignored his cries. They thought it was another joke. Arif tried to save the cows himself, but he could not fight the wolves alone. Many of his cattle were lost, and he got badly injured. Lying had cost him dearly. The next day, the villagers helped him finally, but by then, the damage was done. Arif realized the hard truth: no one trusts a liar, even when he tells the truth. From that day, Arif changed completely. He never lied again. He worked honestly, helped others, and slowly regained the trust of the villagers. Everyone respected him because he learned that honesty is far more valuable than tricks and false stories. Moral: “A liar is never trusted, even when he tells the truth.

text: 7. A story on the lion and the mouse The Lion and the Mouse Once upon a time, in a dense forest, there lived a mighty lion. He was the king of the jungle, feared by all animals. One afternoon, after a big meal, the lion lay under a shady tree and fell asleep. While he was sleeping, a tiny mouse came out of its hole and started playing near the lion. It ran up and down the lion’s back, tickling his ears and tail. Suddenly, the lion woke up with a loud roar. He caught the mouse under his big paw. “How dare you wake me up?” the lion growled. “I will eat you for this!” The little mouse trembled in fear and begged, “Please, mighty lion, let me go. I didn’t mean to disturb you. If you spare my life, I may be able to help you one day!” The lion laughed. “You? A tiny mouse? Help me? That’s the funniest thing I’ve ever heard.” But he felt pity for the little creature and decided to let him go. A few days later, the lion was wandering through the forest when he got caught in a hunter’s net. No matter how hard he tried, he couldn’t escape. He roared loudly for help. Hearing the lion’s roar, the mouse came running. He saw the lion trapped and said, “Don’t worry, I’ll help you.” The mouse began to gnaw at the ropes with his sharp teeth. Slowly and steadily, he chewed through the net until the lion was free. The lion looked at the mouse with great gratitude. “Thank you, little friend. I never thought you could help me, but you saved my life!” The mouse smiled and replied, “Even the smallest friend can make a big difference.” Moral of the story: No act of kindness is ever wasted. /  Even the smallest creature can be of great help.

text: 8. A story on Bayazid's devotion to mother. Bayzid’s Devotion to His Mother Long ago, in a quiet village surrounded by green fields and tall trees, there lived a kind and obedient boy named Bayzid. He was known for his gentle nature, love of learning, and above all, his deep respect and love for his mother. One night, when Bayzid was studying under the dim light of a lamp, he suddenly heard his mother calling from her bed. Her voice was weak, and she asked, “Bayzid, I feel very thirsty. Could you bring me some water?” Bayzid immediately got up and went to fetch water. But when he reached the well, he found that the rope used to draw water was broken. The only other well was quite far from their house. Still, Bayzid didn’t hesitate. He ran through the darkness, barefoot, to fetch water from the distant well. It took him a long time, but finally, he returned with a pot of water. However, when he reached home, he saw that his mother had fallen asleep. Tired but devoted, Bayzid didn’t wake her. Instead, he stood beside her bed, holding the water pot in his hand, waiting patiently for her to wake up. Hours passed. Night turned into morning. Bayzid stood silently the whole time, without moving, without disturbing his mother’s sleep. When his mother finally woke up and saw her son still standing with the water, tears filled her eyes. She was deeply touched by Bayzid’s love and devotion. She raised her hands and prayed, “O Allah, bless my son with great wisdom and honor.” That sincere prayer from a mother’s heart was answered. Bayzid grew up to become a wise and respected man, known as Bayzid Bostami, a great saint of Islam. People still remember him for his deep love for his mother and his devotion to God. Moral: Obeying and respecting one’s parents, especially the mother, brings blessings and honor in this world and the next.
